segments.caption: The video begins with a map of the Balearic Islands (Spain), highlighting Majorca, Minorca, Ibiza, and Formentera, with a red square marking Palma on Majorca. The map transitions to a black background displaying two bones labeled 'MINORCA SPECIES' and 'EUROPEAN RABBIT,' emphasizing size differences. A woman in a light blue top and jeans appears, gesturing as she speaks, with animated silhouettes of a small rabbit and a large, bear-like creature beside her. The scene shifts to a realistic animation of the large creature walking along a rocky beach at sunset, labeled 'NURALAGUS REX' with image credit to Ceri Thomas. The woman reappears, continuing her explanation, while the animated creature is shown again. The video then cuts to a live-action shot of a gray and white rabbit grooming itself in a grassy enclosure with a green fence. The animation of Nuralagus rex reappears, followed by a close-up of a brown and white rodent-like creature on rocks, then a barn owl perched on a branch. The video concludes with the map of the Balearic Islands reappearing, reinforcing the geographical context.

discovered_new_entities.Balearic Islands Map: A detailed map showing Majorca, Minorca, Ibiza, and Formentera, with Palma marked by a red square and labeled 'BALEARIC ISLANDS (SPAIN)'.
discovered_new_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, standing against a black background, gesturing while speaking.
discovered_new_entities.Nuralagus rex: A large, bear-like, rabbit-like creature with a pinkish-brown body and dark legs, shown walking on a rocky beach at sunset, labeled 'NURALAGUS REX'.
discovered_new_entities.European Rabbit: A small, gray and white rabbit shown grooming itself in a grassy enclosure with a green chain-link fence in the background.
discovered_new_entities.Rodent-like Creature: A brown and white rodent with a long tail and pointed snout, shown on rocky terrain in a forested environment.
discovered_new_entities.Barn Owl: A white and brown owl with dark eyes and speckled feathers, perched on a tree branch in a forest setting.
discovered_new_entities.Bone Comparison Image: Two bones displayed on a black background, one large and weathered labeled 'MINORCA SPECIES', the other smaller and white labeled 'EUROPEAN RABBIT', with arrows pointing to each.
discovered_new_entities.Animated Rabbit Silhouette: A small, orange silhouette of a rabbit shown next to the woman presenter, contrasting with the large blue silhouette of Nuralagus rex.
discovered_new_entities.Animated Rodent Silhouette: A brown and white animated rodent shown on rocky terrain, with a forested background, appearing after the barn owl.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Woman Presenter standing against a black textured background, wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, gesturing expressively as she speaks. She introduces a topic related to geography or geology, as the scene transitions to a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, highlighting the sea and surrounding landmasses. The map is shown in detail, with the PBS logo visible in the top left corner. The video then cuts to a close-up of translucent salt crystals, followed by a dramatic underground salt mine with layered, wavy rock formations, where a person in a white hard hat walks through. Another shot shows workers in hard hats observing a salt deposit in a cavern, with the text 'LAKE ASSAL, DJIBOUTI' appearing on screen. The Woman Presenter reappears, continuing her explanation, with a small inset map of the Mediterranean region appearing beside her. The video then returns to the full Mediterranean map, which transitions into a dramatic visualization of the region drying up, with the sea receding and salt deposits forming in intricate patterns. The final frame displays the word 'EONS' in bold white letters over a swirling blue and white abstract background, suggesting a deep-time geological process. Throughout, the PBS logo remains in the top left, and image credits appear for Luca Mariani / Medsalt and the University of Malta / Medsalt for the map recreation.

active_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, standing against a black background, gesturing while speaking.

discovered_new_entities.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea and surrounding landmasses, with the PBS logo in the top left corner, later transitioning to a dried-up version with salt deposits.
discovered_new_entities.Salt Crystals Image: A close-up of translucent, irregularly shaped salt crystals, with a cool blue-white tone, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
discovered_new_entities.Underground Salt Mine: A cavernous salt mine with layered, wavy rock formations in shades of white and gray, with a person in a white hard hat walking through, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
discovered_new_entities.Lake Assal, Djibouti Scene: A cavern with salt deposits and workers in white hard hats observing, with the text 'LAKE ASSAL, DJIBOUTI' displayed at the bottom left, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
discovered_new_entities.Dried Mediterranean Visualization: An animated map showing the Mediterranean Sea receding and forming intricate salt patterns, with text crediting Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt.
discovered_new_entities.EONS Title Card: The word 'EONS' in bold white letters over a swirling blue and white abstract background, suggesting a geological time scale.

segments.caption: The video begins with an animated visualization of the Strait of Gibraltar, showing swirling ocean currents in blue and white, transitioning to a satellite view labeled 'STRAIT OF GIBRALTAR' with the PBS logo. The scene shifts to a serene aerial shot of a coastal wetland with meandering rivers, greenery, and a distant shoreline under a partly cloudy sky. This is followed by a dynamic animated recreation of the Mediterranean region, showing the sea receding and forming salt deposits, credited to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt. The Woman Presenter appears against a black background, gesturing as she speaks, with the text 'MESSINIAN SALINITY CRISIS' displayed. The animation continues, showing the Mediterranean drying up, then transitions to a timeline graphic labeled 'MIOCENE EPOCH' with markers for 23.03 MYA and 5.333 MYA, highlighting the Kapitean-Optian Glacial Event. The video ends with a shot of an underground salt mine, where people in white hard hats observe layered salt formations, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.

active_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, standing against a black background, gesturing while speaking.
active_entities.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea and surrounding landmasses, with the PBS logo in the top left corner, later transitioning to a dried-up version with salt deposits.

active_entities.Underground Salt Mine: A cavernous salt mine with layered, wavy rock formations in shades of white and gray, with a person in a white hard hat walking through, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.

discovered_new_entities.MIOCENE EPOCH Timeline: A dark blue graphic with a light blue horizontal bar labeled 'MIOCENE EPOCH', marked with 23.03 MYA and 5.333 MYA, and a green line indicating the 'KAPITEAN-OPTIAN GLACIAL EVENT'.

segments.caption: The video begins with a stylized animation of an iceberg, showing a small white tip above the water and a much larger submerged portion below, symbolizing the 'tip of the iceberg' concept. This transitions to a realistic, sweeping shot of a rugged glacier in a mountainous region, with snow-covered peaks and crevassed ice fields under a cloudy sky. The scene then shifts to a breathtaking view of Earth from space, half-illuminated by sunlight, showcasing swirling clouds and blue oceans against the blackness of space. A woman presenter, wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, appears against a dark background, gesturing as she speaks, with the Earth image displayed in a white-bordered frame to her right. The video cuts to a close-up of a gloved hand holding a translucent, cylindrical ice core sample in a snowy environment, highlighting its crystalline texture. The presenter reappears, continuing her explanation with expressive hand movements, while the Earth image remains in the frame. The animation of the iceberg reappears, followed by a dynamic visualization of the Mediterranean Sea receding, with blue water patterns flowing back into the ocean, revealing dry land. The final frames show a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, with the sea fully drained, exposing the seabed and surrounding continents, emphasizing the dramatic transformation of the landscape.

discovered_new_entities.Animated Iceberg: A stylized, geometric iceberg with a small white tip above the water and a large submerged portion below, shown in a light blue ocean with wavy surface lines.
discovered_new_entities.Glacier Scene: A realistic, sweeping view of a rugged glacier in a mountainous region, with snow-covered peaks, crevassed ice fields, and rocky outcrops under a cloudy sky.
discovered_new_entities.Earth from Space: A view of Earth from space, half-illuminated by sunlight, showing swirling clouds, blue oceans, and landmasses against the blackness of space, displayed in a white-bordered frame.
discovered_new_entities.Ice Core Sample: A close-up of a gloved hand holding a translucent, cylindrical ice core sample in a snowy environment, showcasing its crystalline texture and internal bubbles.

segments.caption: The video begins with a satellite view of the Mediterranean region, showing the sea and surrounding landmasses, labeled as the 'Mediterranean Map'. The sea then recedes dramatically, revealing a dried-up basin with salt deposits, illustrating the 'Dried Mediterranean Visualization' credited to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt. The scene transitions to the 'Woman Presenter' standing against a dark background, gesturing as she speaks, with a diagram of tectonic plates and a temperature graph appearing beside her. The video then shows a 3D animation of continental drift, credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011, followed by icons representing rain and temperature changes over a grayscale map. A cross-section animation depicts water levels dropping between two landmasses. The scene shifts to a real-life research vessel at sea, where crew members in safety gear operate equipment, credited to Daniel Garcia Castellanos / Medsalt. The final frame returns to the 3D animation of continental collision, showing tectonic plates converging with red magma beneath, again credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011.

active_entities.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea and surrounding landmasses, with the PBS logo in the top left corner, later transitioning to a dried-up version with salt deposits.

discovered_new_entities.Tectonic Plate Diagram: A cross-sectional illustration showing oceanic and continental crust, lithosphere, asthenosphere, trench, and island arc, appearing beside the Woman Presenter.
discovered_new_entities.Temperature Graph: A line graph showing temperature changes over geological time, labeled with epochs like Eocene, Oligocene, and Miocene, displayed below the tectonic plate diagram.
discovered_new_entities.3D Continental Drift Animation: A 3D animated sequence showing the movement and collision of continents, credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011.
discovered_new_entities.Weather Icons: Animated icons of a rain cloud with downward arrow and a thermometer with upward arrow, overlaid on a grayscale map.
discovered_new_entities.Water Level Cross-Section: A simplified animation showing a blue water body between two landmasses, with the water level decreasing over time.
discovered_new_entities.Research Vessel Scene: A real-life shot of a research ship at sea, with crew members in safety gear operating equipment, credited to Daniel Garcia Castellanos / Medsalt.

segments.caption: The video begins with a 3D animated cross-section of tectonic plates, showing molten rock beneath the Earth's crust, credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011. This transitions to a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, highlighting the Red Sea and surrounding landmasses. The scene shifts to the Woman Presenter, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, with a small inset image of a lush riverbank with palm trees beside her. She continues speaking as a graph titled 'MILANKOVITCH CYCLES' appears, displaying three oscillating lines over time (in years before present), credited to INCREDO. The video then shows a simplified diagram of Earth's orbit around the Sun, with graphics based on NASA / Robert Simmon, followed by an animation of Earth’s axial tilt and rotation, labeled with 'GEOGRAPHIC AXIS' and 'SPIN AXIS' against a space reference frame. The tectonic plate animation reappears, followed by the Woman Presenter again, now with a scientific paper citation on 'The Messinian Salinity Crisis' from Marine Geology (2014) displayed beside her. The final segment features a timeline graphic labeled 'MIOCENE EPOCH', spanning from 23.03 MYA to 5.333 MYA, with a highlighted section for the 'MESSINIAN SALINITY CRISIS' at the end, set against a dark, patterned background.

active_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking, with various scientific graphics appearing beside her.
active_entities.3D Continental Drift Animation: A 3D animated sequence showing tectonic plates with molten rock beneath, credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011, appearing at the start and again later in the segment.
active_entities.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea and surrounding landmasses, including the Red Sea, with the PBS logo in the top left corner.
active_entities.MIOCENE EPOCH Timeline: A dark blue graphic with a light blue horizontal bar labeled 'MIOCENE EPOCH', marked with 23.03 MYA and 5.333 MYA, and a highlighted section for the 'MESSINIAN SALINITY CRISIS'.

discovered_new_entities.Milankovitch Cycles Graph: A graph titled 'MILANKOVITCH CYCLES' with three oscillating lines (orange, red, yellow) over time (in years before present), credited to INCREDO.
discovered_new_entities.Earth Orbit Diagram: A simplified diagram showing Earth orbiting the Sun with dashed orbital path and directional arrows, credited to NASA / Robert Simmon.
discovered_new_entities.Earth Axial Tilt Animation: An animation of Earth rotating with labeled 'GEOGRAPHIC AXIS' (blue) and 'SPIN AXIS' (orange) against a space reference frame, illustrating axial precession.
discovered_new_entities.Scientific Paper Citation: A citation for 'The Messinian Salinity Crisis: Past and future of a great challenge for marine sciences' from Marine Geology, Volume 352, June 2014, displayed beside the Woman Presenter.

segments.caption: The video begins with a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, labeled as a 'Dried Mediterranean Visualization' with credits to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt. The map shows the Mediterranean Sea receding, revealing landmasses and intricate salt patterns. This transitions into a simplified cross-sectional animation, 'Water Level Cross-Section,' depicting a blue water body between two landmasses, which gradually evaporates, leaving a thin white layer at the bottom. The scene then shifts to a real-life image of a person in a white hard hat exploring a cavernous salt mine, identified as 'Underground Salt Mine,' with layered rock formations and credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt. The video then cuts to the 'Woman Presenter' standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, with a small inset of the Mediterranean map beside her. She continues her explanation, and the video briefly returns to the satellite map before showing a stunning aerial view of a turquoise coastal bay with rocky cliffs. The presenter reappears, continuing her narration with the map inset still visible, emphasizing the geological transformation of the Mediterranean region.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Woman Presenter standing against a dark textured background, wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, gesturing as she speaks. She appears to be introducing a topic related to marine or geological science. The scene transitions to an underwater shot of a vibrant seagrass meadow with a school of fish swimming above, showcasing a healthy marine ecosystem. This is followed by a close-up of a rocky cliff face, labeled with 'IMAGE CREDIT: VERISIMILUS', suggesting a geological context. The video then cuts to a colorful underwater reef scene featuring a sea urchin, corals, and sponges, emphasizing biodiversity. Returning to the Woman Presenter, she continues speaking, now with an inset image of a spotted sea snail appearing beside her, which she references as she gestures. The scene shifts again to a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, labeled 'MSC RECREATION BY AARON MICALLEF, DANIEL GARCIA-CASTELLANOS, ANGELO CAMERLENGHI / UNIVERSITY OF MALTA / MEDSALT', which then animates to show the sea receding, illustrating the Messinian Salinity Crisis. The map transitions to a dried Mediterranean visualization, highlighting the dramatic geological event. The PBS logo remains visible in the top left corner throughout the segment, maintaining brand consistency.

discovered_new_entities.Sea Snail Image: A close-up inset image of a spotted sea snail with a brown and yellow shell, appearing beside the Woman Presenter.

segments.caption: The video begins with an animated depiction of a dwarf hippopotamus walking along a sandy beach near the ocean, rendered in a stylized, semi-transparent form with a soft, dreamlike quality. The scene transitions to a satellite map of the Mediterranean region, showing the sea receding and forming intricate salt patterns, credited to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt. This is followed by an animated capybara-like creature walking on a rocky shore at sunset, labeled with image credit to Ceri Thomas. Next, a realistic close-up of a brown and white rodent perched on a mossy rock appears, followed by a map highlighting Sicily, Malta, and Sardinia. The dwarf hippopotamus reappears, then a fossilized skull of the Cyprus Dwarf Hippopotamus is shown, labeled accordingly. The Mediterranean map returns, now animated with swirling blue and white patterns representing salt deposits. The scene shifts to a small elephant walking in a grassy field as a large white bird flies overhead, followed by a barn owl perched on a tree branch in a forest. The video maintains a consistent PBS logo in the top left corner and includes image credits in the bottom right for each animated or fossilized scene.

active_entities.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea receding and forming salt patterns, with animated blue and white swirling patterns, credited to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt.

segments.caption: The video opens with a serene, animated scene of a Capybara-like Creature walking along a rocky beach at sunset, its pinkish-brown body glowing softly in the golden light, with gentle waves lapping at the shore. The scene transitions to an aerial view of a rocky outcrop in deep blue ocean waters, with a small white boat nearby, showcasing the vastness and clarity of the sea. The video then cuts to the Woman Presenter standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, with a small inset image of a Mediterranean map appearing beside her. This is followed by a sweeping aerial shot of a coastline with a large cargo ship sailing in the distance, under a partly cloudy sky. The Woman Presenter reappears, continuing her explanation, before the video cuts to a dramatic close-up of a massive, curling ocean wave breaking with force, its turquoise water turning white with foam. An animated cross-section of a water body between two landmasses appears, illustrating a geological concept, before returning to the Woman Presenter. The sequence ends with another intense close-up of crashing waves, followed by the Woman Presenter once more, emphasizing her points with hand gestures. Throughout, the PBS logo is visible in the top left corner, and the lighting shifts from warm sunset tones to cool, dynamic ocean blues and dark studio lighting.

active_entities.Capybara-like Creature: An animated, pinkish-brown, capybara-like animal walking on a rocky beach at sunset, with a soft glow and image credit to Ceri Thomas.
active_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top, blue jeans, and brown boots, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking, with an inset image of a Mediterranean map appearing beside her.

active_entities.Water Level Cross-Section: A simplified animation showing a blue water body between two landmasses, which evaporates to leave a thin white layer at the bottom.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic close-up of churning white water, suggesting a powerful flood or rapid current, setting a dramatic tone. The scene transitions to the Woman Presenter standing against a dark textured background, wearing a light blue long-sleeve top and blue jeans, gesturing as she speaks. The text 'ZANCLEAN FLOOD' appears prominently on screen, indicating the topic. An inset Tectonic Plate Diagram appears beside her, illustrating oceanic and continental crust, lithosphere, asthenosphere, trench, and island arc, as she explains geological processes. The video then cuts to a fossilized skull of a large prehistoric mammal, possibly a mastodon or mammoth, displayed in a museum setting, followed by a close-up of a single fossilized bone against a black background. The scene shifts to a breathtaking aerial view of snow-covered mountain peaks under a clear blue sky, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt. This is followed by an aerial shot of a rocky island in deep blue water, with a small boat nearby. The video then transitions to an animated sequence: a semi-transparent Dwarf Hippopotamus walks along a sandy beach near the ocean, followed by an animated Small Elephant walking through a grassy field with a large white bird flying overhead. The final frame shows a stylized, animated Capybara-like Creature walking on a rocky beach at sunset, with a soft, dreamlike glow. The segment ends with a title card displaying 'EONS' in bold white letters over a swirling blue and white abstract background, suggesting a geological time scale.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Woman Presenter standing against a dark textured background, wearing a light blue long-sleeve top and blue jeans, gesturing as she speaks. The PBS logo appears in the top left corner. The scene transitions to a white screen displaying the PBS Video App logo with text 'Stream the best of PBS' and icons for TV, laptop, tablet, and phone, followed by the URL 'pbs.org/videoapp'. The Woman Presenter reappears, now with the PBS logo in a blue box on the right side of the screen. She continues speaking and gesturing, and the text 'PBS.ORG/VIDEOAPP' appears over a prehistoric scene featuring dinosaurs in a forested environment. The scene shifts to an underwater setting with sharks and fish, where text thanks 'THIS MONTH’S EONTOLOGISTS: PATRICK SEIFERT, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, SEAN DENNIS, HOLLIS, AND STEVE!'. The Woman Presenter returns, and the text 'PATREON.COM/EONS' appears on the left. She gestures again, and a green box on the right states 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!'. The video then displays the end credits over the underwater scene, listing production roles, music, and image credits, including sources like NASA, USGS, and Medsalt. The segment concludes with a full-screen blue background featuring the white PBS logo.

active_entities.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top and blue jeans, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking, with the PBS logo visible in the top left corner and various text overlays appearing beside her throughout the segment.

discovered_new_entities.Underwater Scene: A deep blue underwater environment with sharks and fish swimming, serving as the background for the 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH’S EONTOLOGISTS' text and the end credits.
discovered_new_entities.End Credits Screen: A detailed list of production credits, including names, roles, music, and image sources, displayed over the underwater scene with white text and social media handles for EONS.
discovered_new_entities.PBS Video App Advertisement: A promotional screen with the PBS Video App logo, text 'Stream the best of PBS', device icons, and the URL 'pbs.org/videoapp', shown on a white background.
discovered_new_entities.Thanks to Eontologists Text: White text on a blue underwater background reading 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH’S EONTOLOGISTS: PATRICK SEIFERT, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, SEAN DENNIS, HOLLIS, AND STEVE!'.
discovered_new_entities.Patreon URL Text: White text 'PATREON.COM/EONS' displayed on the left side of the screen while the Woman Presenter speaks.
discovered_new_entities.Studio Credit Box: A green rectangular box with white text stating 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!' appearing on the right side of the screen.
discovered_new_entities.Full-Screen PBS Logo: A solid blue background with the white PBS logo centered, serving as the final frame of the segment.

entity_library.Balearic Islands Map: A detailed map showing Majorca, Minorca, Ibiza, and Formentera, with Palma marked by a red square and labeled 'BALEARIC ISLANDS (SPAIN)'.
entity_library.Woman Presenter: A woman wearing a light blue long-sleeve top and blue jeans, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking, with the PBS logo visible in the top left corner and various text overlays appearing beside her throughout the segment.
entity_library.Nuralagus rex: A large, bear-like, rabbit-like creature with a pinkish-brown body and dark legs, shown walking on a rocky beach at sunset, labeled 'NURALAGUS REX'.
entity_library.European Rabbit: A small, gray and white rabbit shown grooming itself in a grassy enclosure with a green chain-link fence in the background.
entity_library.Rodent-like Creature: A realistic close-up of a brown and white rodent with a long tail and pointed snout, perched on a mossy rock in a forested environment.
entity_library.Barn Owl: A white and brown owl with dark eyes and speckled feathers, perched on a tree branch in a forest setting, with soft lighting and blurred background.
entity_library.Bone Comparison Image: Two bones displayed on a black background, one large and weathered labeled 'MINORCA SPECIES', the other smaller and white labeled 'EUROPEAN RABBIT', with arrows pointing to each.
entity_library.Animated Rabbit Silhouette: A small, orange silhouette of a rabbit shown next to the woman presenter, contrasting with the large blue silhouette of Nuralagus rex.
entity_library.Animated Rodent Silhouette: A brown and white animated rodent shown on rocky terrain, with a forested background, appearing after the barn owl.
entity_library.Mediterranean Map: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea receding and forming salt patterns, with animated blue and white swirling patterns, credited to Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt.
entity_library.Salt Crystals Image: A close-up of translucent, irregularly shaped salt crystals, with a cool blue-white tone, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
entity_library.Underground Salt Mine: A cavernous salt mine with layered, wavy rock formations in shades of white and gray, with a person in a white hard hat walking through, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
entity_library.Lake Assal, Djibouti Scene: A cavern with salt deposits and workers in white hard hats observing, with the text 'LAKE ASSAL, DJIBOUTI' displayed at the bottom left, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
entity_library.Dried Mediterranean Visualization: A satellite map of the Mediterranean region showing the sea receding and forming salt patterns, with text crediting Aaron Micallef, Daniel Garcia-Castellanos, Angelo Camerlenghi / University of Malta / Medsalt.
entity_library.EONS Title Card: The word 'EONS' in bold white letters over a swirling blue and white abstract background, suggesting a geological time scale.
entity_library.MIOCENE EPOCH Timeline: A dark blue graphic with a light blue horizontal bar labeled 'MIOCENE EPOCH', marked with 23.03 MYA and 5.333 MYA, and a highlighted section for the 'MESSINIAN SALINITY CRISIS'.
entity_library.Animated Iceberg: A stylized, geometric iceberg with a small white tip above the water and a large submerged portion below, shown in a light blue ocean with wavy surface lines.
entity_library.Glacier Scene: A realistic, sweeping view of a rugged glacier in a mountainous region, with snow-covered peaks, crevassed ice fields, and rocky outcrops under a cloudy sky.
entity_library.Earth from Space: A view of Earth from space, half-illuminated by sunlight, showing swirling clouds, blue oceans, and landmasses against the blackness of space, displayed in a white-bordered frame.
entity_library.Ice Core Sample: A close-up of a gloved hand holding a translucent, cylindrical ice core sample in a snowy environment, showcasing its crystalline texture and internal bubbles.
entity_library.Tectonic Plate Diagram: A cross-sectional illustration showing oceanic and continental crust, lithosphere, asthenosphere, trench, and island arc, appearing beside the Woman Presenter as she explains geological processes.
entity_library.Temperature Graph: A line graph showing temperature changes over geological time, labeled with epochs like Eocene, Oligocene, and Miocene, displayed below the tectonic plate diagram.
entity_library.3D Continental Drift Animation: A 3D animated sequence showing tectonic plates with molten rock beneath, credited to Garcia-Castellanos @ ICTJA-CSIC, 2011, appearing at the start and again later in the segment.
entity_library.Weather Icons: Animated icons of a rain cloud with downward arrow and a thermometer with upward arrow, overlaid on a grayscale map.
entity_library.Water Level Cross-Section: A simplified animation showing a blue water body between two landmasses, which evaporates to leave a thin white layer at the bottom.
entity_library.Research Vessel Scene: A real-life shot of a research ship at sea, with crew members in safety gear operating equipment, credited to Daniel Garcia Castellanos / Medsalt.
entity_library.Milankovitch Cycles Graph: A graph titled 'MILANKOVITCH CYCLES' with three oscillating lines (orange, red, yellow) over time (in years before present), credited to INCREDO.
entity_library.Earth Orbit Diagram: A simplified diagram showing Earth orbiting the Sun with dashed orbital path and directional arrows, credited to NASA / Robert Simmon.
entity_library.Earth Axial Tilt Animation: An animation of Earth rotating with labeled 'GEOGRAPHIC AXIS' (blue) and 'SPIN AXIS' (orange) against a space reference frame, illustrating axial precession.
entity_library.Scientific Paper Citation: A citation for 'The Messinian Salinity Crisis: Past and future of a great challenge for marine sciences' from Marine Geology, Volume 352, June 2014, displayed beside the Woman Presenter.
entity_library.Underwater Seagrass Scene: A vibrant underwater view of a seagrass meadow with a school of fish swimming above, under clear blue water.
entity_library.Rocky Cliff Face: A close-up of a layered, weathered cliff face with sparse vegetation, credited to VERISIMILUS.
entity_library.Underwater Reef Scene: A colorful underwater reef with a sea urchin, corals, sponges, and other marine life, showcasing biodiversity.
entity_library.Sea Snail Image: A close-up inset image of a spotted sea snail with a brown and yellow shell, appearing beside the Woman Presenter.
entity_library.Dwarf Hippopotamus: An animated, semi-transparent, dark gray hippopotamus walking along a sandy beach near the ocean, with a soft, dreamlike visual style and image credit to Ceri Thomas.
entity_library.Capybara-like Creature: An animated, pinkish-brown, capybara-like animal walking on a rocky beach at sunset, with a soft glow and image credit to Ceri Thomas.
entity_library.Map of Mediterranean Islands: A stylized map highlighting Sicily, Malta, and Sardinia, with surrounding countries labeled, including Tunisia, Greece, and Albania.
entity_library.Cyprus Dwarf Hippopotamus Fossil: A fossilized skull of the Cyprus Dwarf Hippopotamus, shown in a museum-like setting with visible teeth and bone structure, labeled with text.
entity_library.Small Elephant: An animated, gray elephant with small tusks walking through a grassy field, with a large white bird flying overhead, suggesting a prehistoric or fantasy setting.
entity_library.Fossilized Skull: A fossilized skull of a large prehistoric mammal, possibly a mastodon or mammoth, displayed in a museum-like setting with visible teeth and bone structure, labeled with text.
entity_library.Fossilized Bone: A single fossilized bone, light brown and weathered, shown against a black background, with visible cracks and texture.
entity_library.Snow-Covered Mountain Peaks: A sweeping aerial view of rugged, snow-covered mountain peaks under a clear blue sky, with sharp ridges and glaciers, credited to Luca Mariani / Medsalt.
entity_library.Rocky Island in Blue Water: An aerial shot of a rocky island emerging from deep blue water, with a small white boat nearby, showcasing clear turquoise shallows around the rocks.
entity_library.PBS Logo: The white PBS logo (a stylized head silhouette) displayed in the top left corner throughout the segment, and later as a full-screen blue background with the logo centered.
entity_library.PBS Video App Logo: A blue square with the white PBS head silhouette and the text 'PBS VIDEO APP' in bold black letters, accompanied by icons for TV, laptop, tablet, and phone, and the URL 'pbs.org/videoapp'.
entity_library.Underwater Scene: A deep blue underwater environment with sharks and fish swimming, serving as the background for the 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH’S EONTOLOGISTS' text and the end credits.
entity_library.End Credits Screen: A detailed list of production credits, including names, roles, music, and image sources, displayed over the underwater scene with white text and social media handles for EONS.
entity_library.PBS Video App Advertisement: A promotional screen with the PBS Video App logo, text 'Stream the best of PBS', device icons, and the URL 'pbs.org/videoapp', shown on a white background.
entity_library.Thanks to Eontologists Text: White text on a blue underwater background reading 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH’S EONTOLOGISTS: PATRICK SEIFERT, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, SEAN DENNIS, HOLLIS, AND STEVE!'.
entity_library.Patreon URL Text: White text 'PATREON.COM/EONS' displayed on the left side of the screen while the Woman Presenter speaks.
entity_library.Studio Credit Box: A green rectangular box with white text stating 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!' appearing on the right side of the screen.
entity_library.Full-Screen PBS Logo: A solid blue background with the white PBS logo centered, serving as the final frame of the segment.
